searchDocs.title: TerrainConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface TerrainConfig { slice: (BasePart | Region3int16 )?-- Region of terrain to save. If a BasePart is provided it is converted to a Region3int16. When nil, all terrain is saved. }

searchDocs.title: LightingConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface LightingConfig { effectsWhiteList: {string}?-- ClassNames of Lighting effects to include. effectsBlackList: {string}?-- ClassNames of Lighting effects to exclude. }

searchDocs.title: InstanceConfig​
searchDocs.content: </> interface InstanceConfig { ignoreParents: boolean?-- When true, instances are saved without recording their original parent path. }

searchDocs.title: Snapshot​
searchDocs.content: </> Smapshot.Snapshot( config: SnapshotConfig-- Describes what to capture and where to store the result. ) → Folder -- A snapshot containing the serialized map data.

interface SnapshotConfig { name: string?-- Name applied to the returned snapshot Folder. saveLocation: Instance? -- If provided, the snapshot is parented here with a unique name. saveDefaults: boolean?-- When true, properties that match Roblox defaults are still serialized. terrain: TerrainConfig?-- Terrain to capture. Omit to skip terrain. instances: {Instance }?-- Instances to capture. Omit to skip instances. instanceConfig: InstanceConfig?-- Additional options for instance saving. lighting: LightingConfig?-- Lighting settings to capture. Omit to skip lighting. }  Creates a snapshot of terrain, instances, and/or lighting based on the supplied SnapshotConfig. The returned Folder can later be passed to Smapshot.Load to restore the captured state. If config.saveLocation is set, the folder is parented there with a unique name. local snapshot = Smapshot.Snapshot({ name = "Arena", saveLocation = game:GetService("ReplicatedStorage"), terrain = { slice = workspace.ArenaBounds }, instances = workspace.Arena:GetChildren(), lighting = { effectsBlackList = { "Sky" } }, })

searchDocs.title: IsSnapshot​
searchDocs.content: </> Smapshot.IsSnapshot( snapshot: Instance -- The snapshot you are trying to validate. ) → boolean-- The result of the validation. Checks if the input has been made by Smapshot.Snapshot. Smapshot.IsSnapshot(Smapshot.Snapshot({})) -- true Smapshot.IsSnapshot(Instance.new("Folder")) -- false

searchDocs.title: Load​
searchDocs.content: </> Smapshot.Load( snapshot: Folder ,-- The Snapshot to load. keepDirty: boolean?-- When true, existing terrain and lighting state is preserved instead of being cleared first. ) → Folder -- The loaded snapshot. Loads a snapshot folder previously produced by Smapshot.Snapshot. Use Smapshot.LoadAssetAsync when loading from a Roblox asset id instead. Smapshot.Load(snapshot)

searchDocs.title: LoadAssetAsync​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Smapshot.LoadAssetAsync( assetId: number,-- Asset id of an uploaded snapshot. keepDirty: boolean?-- When true, existing terrain and lighting state is preserved instead of being cleared first. ) → Folder -- The loaded snapshot. Loads a snapshot from a Roblox asset id. The asset is fetched via AssetService:LoadAssetAsync and cached for subsequent loads. Use Smapshot.Load when loading from an in-memory snapshot Folder instead. Smapshot.LoadAssetAsync(1234567890)
